title: Notes from the Workshop
synopsis: When a mysterious sound only she can hear echoes through a cluttered inventor's workshop, Sable the baby seal exchanges notes with the eccentric creatures who live there, discovering that working together creates the most beautiful music of all.

pages.text:
A soft ping echoed through the cluttered workshop, somewhere between the sound of a raindrop and a tiny bell.

Sable lifted her silver whiskers from the pile of copper springs she'd been sorting. The sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur and huge dark liquid eyes looked around the towering shelves of half-finished inventions. Gears hung from strings like metal fruit. Paintbrushes sprouted from jam jars full of colorful goop. A mechanical bird sat frozen mid-tweet, one gear missing from its chest.

Ping.

There it was again. Sable's long silver whiskers twitched. She slapped her flippers together once, twice. "Did anyone else hear that?"

Cricket chirped from somewhere inside a grandfather clock that ran backwards. "Hear what, Sable?"

"The ping sound!"

"I only hear my own beautiful chirping," Cricket called back.

Sable grabbed a stubby pencil in her flipper and found a scrap of brown paper.

illustration.description: A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers holds a tiny pencil while surrounded by towering shelves of bizarre inventions. Musical notes float in the air around her. The workshop is a candy-colored chaos of gears, springs, and half-built contraptions. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with squash and stretch, oversized expressions, bouncy dynamic energy, and rubber hose animation feel.
illustration.alt: Sable the baby seal writing in a workshop full of inventions

pages.text:
Dear Cricket,

I keep hearing a ping sound. It's musical and lovely. Are you sure you don't hear it?

From,
Sable

P.S. - I left this note in the purple teapot.

---

Dear Sable,

I checked my ears. They work fine. I heard Gus the mechanical mouse practicing his tap dancing in the biscuit tin. I heard the wind chimes made of bottle caps. But no ping.

Maybe try asking Ophelia?

From,
Cricket

---

Ping.

Sable's whiskers drooped. The sound was getting stronger, more frequent. It seemed to bounce off the workshop's wooden walls like a rubber ball made of music. She waddled over to where Ophelia the owl lived in a nest built inside an old gramophone horn.

illustration.description: A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers drops a note into a bright purple teapot. Around the workshop, a tiny cricket peeks out from a backwards grandfather clock while musical ping sounds float visibly in the air as sparkly notes. The scene has exaggerated cartoon style with candy bright colors and slapstick energy.
illustration.alt: Sable leaving a note in a purple teapot for Cricket

pages.text:
Dear Ophelia,

There's a ping sound only I can hear. Cricket can't hear it. Can you? You have the best ears in the workshop.

Your friend,
Sable

---

Dear Small Seal,

I hear many things. The settling of wood beams. The tick of seventeen broken clocks. The whisper of dust settling on forgotten inventions. But I hear no ping.

Have you considered that this sound might be meant especially for you?

Wisely yours,
Ophelia

---

Sable read Ophelia's note three times. Meant especially for her? The ping came again, and this time she noticed something new. It had a rhythm. Ping-ping-pause-ping. Like a song with missing pieces.

She grabbed her pencil and wrote furiously.

illustration.description: A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers writes at the base of a gramophone horn where a wise owl peers down. The workshop around them is filled with broken clocks showing different times and piles of dusty inventions. Musical ping notes float in patterns around the seal. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with oversized expressions and comedic poses.
illustration.alt: Sable writing a letter to Ophelia the owl who lives in a gramophone

pages.text:
Dear Everyone,

I figured it out! The ping has a rhythm: ping-ping-pause-ping.

Cricket, could you chirp in the pauses?
Ophelia, could you hoot a low note under it all?
Gus, could you tap along?

Let's meet by the big copper funnel at sunset.

Musically yours,
Sable

---

Ping-ping-pause-ping went Sable's mysterious sound.
Chirp-chirp-chirp-chirp went Cricket in the gaps.
Whoo-whoo-whoo-whoo hummed Ophelia underneath.
Tappa-tappa-tap-tap clicked Gus the mechanical mouse.

The workshop filled with their strange, wonderful music. Gears seemed to shimmer. Springs bounced in time. Even the frozen mechanical bird's eyes seemed to sparkle.

"Now I hear it too!" Cricket exclaimed.
"The ping was calling us to play together," Ophelia observed wisely.
"Best practice session ever!" squeaked Gus.

illustration.description: A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers conducts a musical ensemble with her flippers. Around a large copper funnel, a cricket, an owl in a gramophone, and a tiny mechanical mouse all make music together. Colorful musical notes fill the air in swirling patterns. The workshop inventions seem to shimmer and dance. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with bouncy dynamic energy and candy bright colors.
illustration.alt: Sable conducting a musical group with her workshop friends

pages.text:
Dear Workshop Friends,

Thank you for making music with me. I think the ping sound was lonely, just like I was when I first arrived here among all these wonderful broken inventions.

Sounds are better when they're shared.

Love,
Sable

---

Dear Musical Seal,

Every evening now, we gather by the copper funnel. Our workshop symphony grows stronger each day. Cricket learned to chirp in harmony. Gus invented a tap-dance that matches the ping perfectly. And I discovered that my hoots make everything sound richer.

The ping still chooses you to hear it first. But now you help all of us join in.

That makes you our conductor.

With respect,
Ophelia, Cricket, and Gus

---

That night, as their music filled the cluttered workshop, Sable realized something wonderful. The mysterious ping hadn't been calling for attention. It had been calling for friends. And the most beautiful sounds happened when everyone played their part, together, in perfect imperfect harmony.

illustration.description: A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers sits contentedly in the center of the workshop as musical notes swirl around all the inventions. Cricket, Ophelia, Gus and other small creatures are positioned throughout the space, all contributing to a symphony that makes the entire cluttered room glow with warm light. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with slapstick energy and rubber hose animation feel.
illustration.alt: Sable and friends making music that fills the entire magical workshop

morals: Working together creates something more beautiful than working alone
Everyone has something unique to contribute
Listening to others makes us stronger

coverPrompt.description: A sleek baby seal with silver-grey fur, huge dark liquid eyes, and long silver whiskers sits among towering piles of gears, springs, and half-built contraptions in a wonderfully chaotic workshop. Musical notes float in the air around her flippers as she holds a tiny pencil. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with candy bright colors, bouncy dynamic energy, and a rubber hose animation feel.
coverPrompt.alt: Sable the baby seal writing notes in a cluttered inventor's workshop

questions.question: What sound does only Sable hear at first?
questions.explanation: Sable is the only one who can hear the mysterious ping sound in the workshop.

questions.question: How do the friends communicate in the workshop?
questions.explanation: The characters write notes and letters to each other throughout the story.

questions.question: Who lives inside the gramophone horn?
questions.explanation: Ophelia the owl has built her nest inside the old gramophone horn.

questions.question: What does Ophelia think the ping sound might be?
questions.explanation: Ophelia wisely suggests that the ping sound might be meant especially for Sable.

questions.question: What happens when all the friends make music together?
questions.explanation: When everyone contributes their unique sounds, they create a beautiful symphony together.
